data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="LYRICSEASON-031225-27.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c7d643f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2F10%2F7023e82845149deae5b74658e1c0%2Flyricseason-031225-27.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4b9bff7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2F10%2F7023e82845149deae5b74658e1c0%2Flyricseason-031225-27.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/c7d643f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6122x3436+0+324/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F71%2F10%2F7023e82845149deae5b74658e1c0%2Flyricseason-031225-27.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Enrique Mazzola, Lyric Opera music director, (left), and John Mangum, the company’s general director, seen in 2025. On Thursday, the company announced its upcoming season, which will include seven operas.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Anthony Vazquez/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Following “Don Giovanni,” Chicago audiences will get to see Giddens and Abels’ “Omar,” in October. The Lyric co-commission won the Pulitzer Prize for Music in 2023. It draws inspiration from Omar ibn Said’s 19th-century memoir. The Muslim scholar was born in West Africa, then enslaved for decades in the American South. The work’s score incorporates hymns, spirituals and bluegrass to create “a tapestry as emotionally direct as it is musically breathtaking,” according to the Lyric.</p><p>Mangum said the presentation of “Omar” is part of the company’s acknowledgement of the country’s <a class="Link" href="https://www.wbez.org/arts-culture/2026/04/02/america-250-illinois-chicago-federal-national-endowment-arts-trump" target="_blank" ><u>250th birthday</u></a>. “We want to look at the complexities of our nation’s history,” Mangum said.</p><p>In November, Lyric will present Gaetano Donizetti’s “Don Pasquale” and two performances of “Wagner: Myth and Music,” which brings the full force of the company’s orchestra and chorus to the stage for a concert featuring tales of valiant heroes and ghostly figures.</p><p>Mazzola will return with Joseph Haydn’s oratorio, “The Creation” in January, featuring an English language libretto, which brings bits of Genesis, the Psalms and John Milton’s “Paradise Lost.”</p><p>The spring mainstage lineup will also include Richard Strauss’ “Ariadne auf Naxos,” “La traviata,” and, finally, “Susannah” in April, which returns to Lyric for the first time since 2002, aligned to mark the centennial of Carlisle Floyd’s birth. Radvanovsky will appear in concert with tenor SeokJong Baek April 30-May 2.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-c40000" name="image-c40000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/100421e/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/95765f0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6e00119/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="2 Omar - c Cory Weaver - LA Opera.jpg" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6e00119/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5945b6f/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6e00119/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6000x3367+0+316/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fd0%2Fff%2F889fe9424653b0e1bfd3bce34012%2F2-omar-c-cory-weaver-la-opera.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>“Omar,” a Lyric co-commission, will make its Chicago premiere in October. The work by Rhiannon Giddens and Michael Abels won the Pulitzer Prize for Music in 2023.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Cory Weaver</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>The season will close with “Guys and Dolls,” reviving a Lyric tradition of showing American musical theater. The production will be led by director Francesca Zambello, conductor James Lowe and choreographer Joshua Bergasse. At a season announcement Thursday, Mangum stopped short of committing to presenting a musical annually going forward.</p><p>“What I love about when an opera company does a musical, it’s done to the absolute highest artistic standard,” Mangum said. “From the casting, sets, costumes to the choreography, you really are getting a superlative musical and dramatic experience.”</p><p>Lyric will also continue its movie series, which screens films as the score is performed live. The first will feature Disney’s “Encanto” in November with original songs by Lin-Manuel Miranda. And, in January, the company will screen “Amadeus,” which will also feature the Lyric Chorus.</p><h3>Lyric Opera of Chicago’s 2026-27 season:</h3><p>“Don Giovanni,” Oct. 10-Nov. 1<br>“Omar,” Oct. 23-28<br>“Don Pasquale,” Nov. 12-27<br>“Wagner: Myth & Music,” Nov. 20 and 22<br>“Encanto” live to film, Nov 28-29<br>“Amadeus” live to film, Jan. 15-16, 2027<br>Haydn’s “The Creation,” Jan. 22-23, 2027<br>“Hérodiade: An opera in concert,” Feb. 23-28, 2027<br>“Ariadne auf Naxos,” March 11-26, 2027<br>“La traviata,” March 24-April 25, 2027<br>“Susannah,” April 11-23, 2027<br>Sondra Radvanovsky and SeokJong Baek in Concert, April 30 and May 2, 2027<br>“Guys and Dolls,” June 4-20, 2027</p><p><i>Courtney Kueppers is an arts and culture reporter at WBEZ. </i></p>
entries.author: Courtney Kueppers | WBEZ

entries.title: Sky reportedly in talks to trade Ariel Atkins for Rickea Jackson
entries.summary: <p>The first bit of intrigue in the post-Angel Reese era has arrived.</p><p>The <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky" target="_blank" >Sky</a> are in talks with the Sparks on a trade that would send Ariel Atkins to Los Angeles for guard Rickea Jackson, according to Annie Costabile of Front Office Sports.</p><p>If the report holds, the Sky would add another young building block to a team that sorely needs scoring and star potential. But they would lose a proven, two-time All-Star, and potentially more, depending on how the deal shakes out. </p><p>Start with the good news. Jackson, 25, is a dynamic up-and-coming player. She averaged 14.7 points per game for the Sparks last season and has the versatility to guard multiple positions. </p><p>Atkins was the Sky's best guard a year ago — and is the better defender — but she struggled to adapt to life as a primary scorer. At 29, she's also on a different timeline than Kamilla Cardoso, the Sky's best piece right now. Jackson might fit better.</p><p>The Sky did not immediately respond to a request for comment, though a source familiar with the team's thinking previously told the Sun-Times they're open to moving Atkins. The Sky designated her as a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky/2026/04/07/sky-extend-core-qualifying-offer-to-guard-ariel-atkins" >core player this week</a>, which would allow them to execute a sign-and-trade. It's still unclear whether Atkins wants to return or wants out — toward the end of a tumultuous 10-34 season, she told the Sun-Times she was undecided.</p><p>If the Sky are able to land Jackson in return, they may have something brewing.</p><p>A healthy Courtney Vandersloot running the show, an ascending Rickea Jackson and a breakout Kamilla Cardoso might be fun to watch. Throw a star free agent into the mix and it's not a bad vision.</p><p>Then again, there are still plenty of ways this trade gets complicated. If the Sky have to include their No. 5 pick in the 2026 draft, the value proposition gets a lot murkier.</p><p>Consider that acquiring Atkins already cost the Sky the No. 3 pick in the 2025 draft — which became Sonia Citron — plus a pick swap in 2027.</p><p>So here's how the final ledger would shake out:</p><p>The Sky get Rickea Jackson.</p><p>But they could have had: the No. 3 pick in the 2025 draft (Sonia Citron), the No. 5 pick in the 2026 draft (Kiki Rice and Flau'jae Johnson are possibilities), and — if they struggle again this year — a potential lottery pick in 2027, the draft class of Juju Watkins, Hannah Hidalgo and Madison Booker.</p><p>Worth noting: the Sky could have drafted Jackson themselves. In 2024, they selected Cardoso with the No. 3 pick. The Sparks took Jackson one spot later at No. 4.</p><p>The Sky then drafted Reese at No. 7, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky/2026/04/07/the-bright-side-of-the-angel-reese-trade-still-looks-dim" >creating a frontcourt pairing that never quite clicked.</a></p>
entries.author: Alissa Hirsh

entries.title: One dead, two hurt in Streamwood house fire
entries.summary: <p>One person is dead and another was transported to a hospital following an early morning house fire in Streamwood Thursday that also injured a firefighter.</p><p>Investigators are still at the scene of the fire that was reported just after 4:45 a.m. on the 100 block of Southwood Circle.</p><p>Streamwood Fire Chief Michael Meyer said heavy smoke and fire was showing from both floors of the single-family, split-level home when firefighters arrived.</p><p>Meyer said one resident was pronounced dead at the scene and a second was transported to a hospital, but their condition is unknown.</p><p>For more on this story, please visit<a class="Link" href="https://www.dailyherald.com/20260409/news/one-dead-two-hurt-in-streamwood-house-fire/" target="_blank" > DailyHerald.com</a>.</p>
entries.author: Daily Herald

entries.title: Rainbow PUSH names new leader after death of founder Rev. Jesse Jackson
entries.summary: <p></p><p>Rainbow PUSH announced Thursday that Yusef Jackson, the youngest son of the Rev. Jesse Jackson Sr., would take over leadership of the coalition as president and CEO.</p><p>The organization's board of directors unanimously approved Yusef Jackson to take the reins, fulfilling his father’s wishes to designate his successor before his death. For the last two years, Yusef Jackson has served as the organization's chief operating officer.</p><p>“I am deeply grateful to my father for his trust, his guidance, and the example he set for all of us,” Yusef Jackson said in a statement.</p><p>The late reverend founded the civil rights organization in 1971 to address the social, cultural and economic needs of diverse communities, while advocating for the rights of disenfranchised Americans.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/obituaries/2026/02/17/rev-jesse-jackson-civil-rights-leader-rainbow-push-obituary" target="_blank" >Rev. Jesse Jackson, civil rights leader and Chicago icon, dead at 84</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/obituaries/2026/02/17/rev-jesse-jackson-civil-rights-leader-rainbow-push-obituary" target="_blank" >Jesse Jackson died Feb. 17</a> at age 84, surrounded by his family. The reverend had been in declining health for more than a decade. In 2017, he announced he had been diagnosed with Parkinson’s disease two years earlier, but last April he revealed the diagnosis was actually progressive supranuclear palsy, a condition that also affects bodily movement. He was also hospitalized just months before he died.</p><p>A day after the reverend’s passing, Yusef Jackson said he would be <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago/2026/02/18/family-of-rev-jesse-jackson-vow-to-continue-fight-for-justice-and-equality-thats-our-calling" target="_blank" >taking over leadership</a> of the coalition. Yusef Jackson said his brother, Jesse Jackson Jr., would also help lead the organization.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure Jesse Jackson at his funeral service on March 6, 2026.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ashlee Rezin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“My father’s leadership and legacy have left an indelible mark on our world," Yusef Jackson said in his Thursday statement. "I had the profound privilege of working alongside him as he tirelessly advocated for human rights and economic justice for all. His work to educate, register, and mobilize millions of voters helped transform our democracy."</p><p>The reverend’s death drew crowds from across the country to the city for weeks-long services honoring and celebrating his legacy. Homegoing services also drew celebrities and former presidents alike, including former presidents Barack Obama, Joe Biden and Bill Clinton.</p><p>At the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/live/jesse-jacksons-chicago-funeral-live-updates#0000019c-c469-dd16-adbc-c57f52900000" target="_blank" >reverend’s funeral service</a>, Yusef Jackson said his father spent his last months urging him to mobilize churches to feed those who lost food assistance during a historic government shutdown.</p><p>“I intend to die with my shoes on,” he recalled his father saying.</p><p>Yusef Jackson ended his message by recalling his father’s famous words: “Keep hope alive.”</p><p>Jesse Jackson <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2023/7/14/23794835/rev-jesse-jackson-rainbow-push-president-stepping-down" target="_blank" >stepped down</a> as leader of the coalition in 2023 due to health concerns and named the Rev. Frederick Haynes III as his successor. But <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago/2024/04/16/new-leader-of-jesse-jacksons-rainbow-push-coalition-steps-down-less-than-3-months-on-the-job" target="_blank" >Hayes resigned</a> less than three months later without saying why.</p>
entries.author: Cindy Hernandez

entries.title: Representante Jesús ‘Chuy’ García anuncia el proceso para renombrar la oficina postal César Chávez
entries.summary: <p>El representante Jesús “Chuy” García alentó el miércoles a los estudiantes de las escuelas secundarias locales a enviar sugerencias para el cambio de nombre de la oficina de correos del vecindario de Pilsen, que actualmente lleva el nombre de César Chávez.</p><p>El plan de participación comunitaria, titulado “Nuestra comunidad, nuestro legado”, está diseñado como una oportunidad educativa en el salón de clases para que los estudiantes discutan posibles nominados para reemplazar a Chávez.</p><p>El cambio de nombre planeado se produce después de una <a class="Link" href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/18/us/cesar-chavez-sexual-abuse-allegations-ufw.html" target="_blank" >investigación del New York Times</a> que descubrió el historial de abuso sexual del prominente activista latino hacia jóvenes y mujeres, incluida la líder Dolores Huerta.</p><p>Chavez, quien murió hace más de tres décadas, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2026/03/19/acusaciones-de-abuso-de-cesar-chavez-entristecen-y-sorprenden-a-la-comunidad-latina-de-chicago" target="_blank" >ha sido durante mucho tiempo un ícono de los derechos civiles</a> para las comunidades latinas y laborales de Chicago. Es honrado con varios murales, escuelas y placas en toda la ciudad, pero ha habido un creciente clamor para reevaluar esos homenajes.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-d5fb66" name="image-d5fb66"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/51b2216/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a7cb0a7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ca441a0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="A plaque inside the Post Office in Pilsen honoring late labor leader Cesar Chavez. February 7, 2020." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ca441a0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/795b2d0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/ca441a0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Ffd%2F81%2F062716a355a3b083f5d1ae25ad83%2Fcesar-chavez-post-office.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Una placa dentro de la Oficina de Correos en Pilsen rinde homenaje al fallecido líder laboral César Chávez.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Carlos Ballesteros/Archivo Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>“El congresista considera que, dadas las revelaciones sobre César Chávez, la oficina postal de Pilsen necesitaba ser renombrada”, dijo un portavoz de García.</p><p>García no es el único que está tomando medidas tras conocer las noticias sobre el acoso y abuso sexual de Chávez. Una serie de murales de Chávez en Chicago <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz/2026/03/26/se-quitan-mas-murales-y-monumentos-de-cesar-chavez-mientras-el-senado-de-illinois-honra-a-dolores-huerta" target="_blank" >han sido retirados o reemplazados</a> por diseños que honran a Huerta.</p><p>El Senado de Illinois adoptó resoluciones para reemplazar el Día de César Chávez por el Día de los trabajadores agrícolas el 31 de marzo y declarar el 10 de abril como “Día de Dolores Huerta” en Illinois.</p><p>“En los últimos meses, Dolores Huerta ha mostrado una profunda resiliencia y valentía al compartir su propia experiencia de daño, haciéndolo para elevar las historias de innumerables mujeres cuyas voces fueron pasadas por alto o silenciadas”, indica la resolución.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-e1efde" name="image-e1efde"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5d8b12b/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F01%2Fbf30f8794d4b8971ea488f80e923%2Fimg-4659.JPG alt="IMG_4659.JPG" srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6d055b7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F01%2Fbf30f8794d4b8971ea488f80e923%2Fimg-4659.JPG 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/276c8f5/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F01%2Fbf30f8794d4b8971ea488f80e923%2Fimg-4659.JPG 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/6d055b7/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fa5%2F01%2Fbf30f8794d4b8971ea488f80e923%2Fimg-4659.JPG" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Un mural que representa a Dolores Huerta, la aliada de César Chávez en el movimiento por los derechos laborales, ha sido pintado sobre un mural que representaba a Chávez fuera del Café Tola en el vecindario de Lake View.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Ari Soglin/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>No está claro cuántos otros sitios en Chicago eliminarán el nombre de Chávez de sus títulos.</p><p>Una primaria pública en el vecindario de Back of the Yards, el César Chávez Multicultural Academic Center, ha iniciado el proceso que podría llevar a un nuevo nombre, según funcionarios de las Escuelas Públicas de Chicago (CPS).</p><p>Cualquier recomendación necesitará la aprobación del Consejo Escolar Local (LSC) y de la junta de educación. El cambio de nombre de la oficina de correos de Pilsen cae dentro de la jurisdicción de García debido a su estatus como edificio federal, pero el cambio de nombre de edificios privados o estatales está fuera del alcance de la autoridad del congresista.</p><p>“Esta oficina de correos es una parte clave de nuestra comunidad, y los miembros de la comunidad deberían proporcionar propuestas para el nuevo nombre”, escribió García en un comunicado de prensa. “Estoy emocionado por escuchar las ideas de los jóvenes en nuestro distrito”.</p><p>Los nominados deben haber fallecido, tener una conexión o contribución clara a Pilsen o al Distrito 4 y deben haber demostrado un compromiso con el servicio público, la educación, el trabajo o los derechos civiles, según García. El programa comenzó el 8 de abril y la fecha límite para que las escuelas participen es el 15 de mayo.</p><p><i>Traducido con una herramienta de inteligencia artificial (AI) y editado por </i><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz" target="_blank" ><i>La Voz Chicago</i></a></p>
entries.author: Zoe Singer

entries.title: Michelada Fest announces full lineup with an added free day after last year’s cancellations
entries.summary: <p><a class="Link" href="https://www.michefest.live/" target="_blank" ><u>Michelada Fest</u></a> organizers announced Thursday that the weekend festival is returning to Chicago after <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/entertainment-culture/2025/05/06/michelada-fest-canceled-chicago-2025-oakwood-beach" target="_blank" ><u>last year’s event was </u></a>canceled amid fears around the “political climate” and issues with visas for foreign artists.</p><p>Happening June 26-28 at its new location Union Park, the lineup includes genre-blending Latin Mafia, Puerto Rican rapper and singer Jhayco, singer-songwriter Jessie Reyez and Jamaican rapper and singer Sean Paul. Michelada Fest, known simply as Miche Fest, celebrates Latin music, art, food and drink, especially the fest’s namesake Mexican cocktail: the michelada.</p><p>More than a dozen local and national artists will perform, including Mexican regional band Dareyes de la Sierra, producer Ovy on the Drums and singer Paloma Mami. Several local artists, such as DJ Eva Maria and Las Vibras de Chicago, will also take the stage that weekend. </p><p>Organizers are also expanding the festival to three days this year. The first day of the festival, on Friday, headlined by DannyLux and Herencia de Patrones, will be free to enter.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-d80000" name="image-d80000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/a6874a0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/e2c74c9/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/407dfdb/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Latin artist Dannylux played to a crowd of fervent fans at Lollapalooza in 2022." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/407dfdb/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/34276e8/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/407dfdb/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6083x3414+0+321/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Fc6%2Fa0%2F8a4cc1324800b4589e7ceb1653cf%2Flolladay3-073122-09.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Latin artist Dannylux performs at Lollapalooza in 2022. The singer and songwriter is headlining the first day of Miche Fest.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Kate Scott/For the Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Fans will notice that there are fewer international artists on this year’s bill, and that was done intentionally, said Miche Fest founder Fernando Nieto.</p><p>Organizers cited problems with international artist visas, as well as the “rapidly changing political climate,” among the reasons for the cancellation last year.</p><p>Immigration enforcement is another concern for organizers. On Sept. 16, the Trump administration began its deportation campaign with Operation Midway Blitz, which put Chicago and the suburbs on high alert, especially in Latino and immigrant-majority neighborhoods.</p><p>“It's something that's at the top of our mind,” Nieto said. “We wanna not only protect our [fans] but also our staff and our vendors who might have undocumented workers. … We're really proud to take these additional necessary steps.”</p><p>Nieto added that Miche Fest volunteers and staff will be trained in de-escalation tactics if problems between law enforcement and artists, vendors or fans arise.</p><p>“We felt that our culture was under attack and they were targeting not only the community but artists and music in general,” Nieto said. “We had four to five artists lose their visas within two to three weeks of us canceling.”</p><p>For this year’s festival, Miche Fest organizers made the conscious decision to book artists whose visas are in good standing.</p><p>“Unfortunately, it did limit and change some of our approach, but we feel we have a really cool and strong lineup,” Nieto continued. “It was the safest path forward to building the festival lineup so that we wouldn't run into that issue for this year.”</p><p>After organizers canceled last year’s event, they focused on building a coalition of strong partnerships for the relaunch.</p><p>That includes working with Chicago artist <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/murals-mosaics/2022/1/28/22894092/sentrock-murals-chicago-fulton-market-joseph-perez" target="_blank" ><u>Sentrock</u></a>, who’s in charge of art installations and an “artist’s world” on the festival grounds, Nieto said. Chicago DJ collective <a class="Link" href="https://www.instagram.com/sentido.wav/" target="_blank" ><u>Sentido</u></a> is curating local artists to perform on an added second stage, and Chicago and Durango-based fashion brand Gente Fina is setting up an exclusive on-site merch pop-up.</p><p>The festival will feature 14 different michelada vendors, as well as plenty of local food options.</p><p>This year’s festival also has a new location: After years of the organizers working to move it to the West Side, Miche Fest will now be held at Union Park, 1501 W. Randolph St. The festival moved to Oakwood Beach from Pilsen in 2024<a class="Link" href="https://southsideweekly.com/5th-pilsen-michelada-festival/" target="_blank" ><u> after residents raised complaints</u></a> over the amount of foot traffic, noise and parking issues brought to the neighborhood.</p><p>“Transparently, we've been trying to get into Union Park for the last few years,” Nieto said. Logistically, he said, operating the festival at Oakwood Beach was a bit of a logistical “headache.”</p><p>Union Park’s proximity to the Green and Pink lines and several bus routes, plus the presence of several parking lots in the area, make it an ideal location, Nieto said.</p><p>“It is gonna make our lives a million times easier,” he said. While Miche Fest doesn’t have a multi-year deal with Union Park, a spokesperson said they intend to make it the festival’s home for years to come.</p><p>In a nod to its community-oriented roots, day one of the festival will be a free-entry day, Nieto said. Instead of tickets, attendees are encouraged to give a suggested donation of $10 to La Casa Norte, a not-for-profit organization for Chicagoans facing housing insecurity.</p><p>“We know that not everybody can afford to attend, so this is for them. This is for the community, this is for accessibility and inclusivity,” Nieto said.</p><p>Tickets are on sale now at <a class="Link" href="http://michefest.live" target="_blank" ><u>MicheFest.live</u></a> and start at $89 for Saturday and Sunday. Children under five years old get free admission with a ticketed adult.</p>
entries.author: Ambar Colón

entries.title: Rush Medical Center nurses petition to form union
entries.summary: <p>Wearing bright red shirts and scrubs, a crowd of Rush University Medical Center nurses gathered Thursday in the heart of Chicago’s medical district to declare their efforts to form a union.</p><p>“We are the nurses,” they chanted as motorists driving by honked in support. “Fighting for our patients, fighting for our community.”</p><p>The nurses, who will be represented by the National Nurses United, filed a petition Wednesday to hold a union election, one of the first steps to formally form a union and bargain a contract. They held a rally outside Rush Medical Center on the Near West Side Thursday where they were joined by other area nurses, labor leaders and local politicians urging the hospital to cooperate with a speedy election.</p><p>“Long live the union,” said Don Villar, of the Chicago Federation of Labor, in Tagalog to the crowd. He shared how Rush nurses helped his late wife when she was treated there for cancer.</p><p>In a statement, Rush said it was aware of the petition and would be discussing it with the nursing team over the next “several weeks.”</p><p>“At Rush, one of our greatest strengths is the way nurses, leaders and care teams work together as colleagues — raising concerns, addressing challenges and making improvements — directly and transparently. The most successful models for the delivery of health care empower individuals to raise issues in real time and to have them addressed at the department level,” the statement read.</p><p>Rush and the union did not specify how many nurses would be eligible to vote for a union but the National Labor Relations Board stated the number of employees was 1,700, and information indicated the unit would include full time, part time and per diem registered nurses. A date for the election has not yet been set.</p><p>If the nurses are successful, they will join more than 6,500 unionized nurses in Chicago area hospitals who are already represented by the National Nurses Organizing Committee, an affiliate of the National Nurses United. Other nurses work at Cook County Health, the University of Chicago Medicine and the Jesse Brown Veterans Administration Medical Center.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/243fff2/2147483647/strip/true/crop/6681x3750+0+352/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2Faf%2F4d%2F15c9c89a423f8f2205c6711ee63d%2Frush-260410-3.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Kira Pilatejean (second from right), an orthopedic nurse, raises her fist during the rally outside Rush University Medical Center.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Pat Nabong/Sun-Times</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Kira Pilatejean, a Rush nurse and union unit leader, said she was excited to be part of a wave of unionization taking place among nurses across the country in places like California, Michigan and Iowa. She said a union would help prevent burnout.</p><p>“This is just protections for us, for our patients to make sure that we stay because I think everybody here loves their job,” Pilatejean said. “We want to stay at our job, and that’s our goal.”</p><p>Many of the nurses Thursday stressed that their efforts are tied to patient safety, saying they need to be involved in conversations around staffing ratios and retention.</p><p>Keeley Binion, who has worked at Rush for 40 years, said the union was important not just for current nurses but for those who will one day work at the hospital. Speaking to the crowd, Binion urged Rush management to partner with them.</p><p>“They need to recognize and respect our rights and patient’s rights and let us have a seat at the table. Let me correct that — we will demand a seat at the table,” Binion said as the crowd cheered.</p>
entries.author: Elvia Malagón

entries.title: Man slain in Garfield Park neighborhood apartment
entries.summary: <p>A Thursday autopsy found a 31-year-old man was fatally assaulted in a Garfield Park neighborhood apartment, officials said.</p><p>Police found Davonta F. Curtis unresponsive with a gunshot wound to the head inside a home in the 3100 block of West Madison Street about 9:15 a.m. Wednesday, Chicago police said.</p><p>But Curtis, who was pronounced dead on the scene, died of multiple blunt force injuries during an assault, according to the Cook County medical examiner's office. Curtis, whose death was ruled a homicide, was of the 2600 block of South Kedvale Avenue, the office said.</p><p>Detectives are investigating.<br></p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: DNC condemns dark money influence but avoids taking stance on AIPAC
entries.summary: <p>A Democratic National Committee panel on Thursday rejected a resolution to condemn the involvement of the American Israel Public Affairs Committee in Democratic races, which specifically called out their nearly $14 million in spending in Illinois primary races.</p><p>DNC members avoided targeting the influential group that has caused major division within the party and instead chose to approve a broader resolution condemning dark money, and calling for "robust" transparency in campaign finance and a commitment to practices "that align with the Party's core values." Members earlier amended a resolution that specifically targeted AI and cryptocurrency spending to more broadly condemn special interest spending.</p><p>The vote by the resolutions committee came after special interest groups spent tens of millions of dollars to influence five congressional primary races in the Chicago area — and after Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker, who is widely seen as a potential 2028 presidential contender, denounced AIPAC for its current ties to Republican donors.</p><p>DNC Member Lottie Shackelford told the panel that she believed the committee had already taken a "very strong position" in opposing dark money in Democratic primary elections through a resolution that passed in August 2025 and led to a reform task force.</p><p>"I don't think at this time we should consider continuous resolutions on one subject after we've already taken a very strong and firm position on the underlying issue," Shackelford said. "Our committee has also expressed its view on the corrosive impact of dark money in politics, including in Democratic primaries, and we have also agreed that it is not efficient to consider individual organizations one by one because there are too many to name, and it will consume the entirety of the committee's work."</p><p>The DNC is currently meeting in New Orleans, where cities that are vying for the 2028 and 2032 Democratic National conventions, including Chicago, are also hosting events for DNC members. Atlanta, Boston, Denver and Philadelphia are also trying to secure the bid.</p><p>Sen. Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., applauded the dark money resolution, despite it's lack of specificity, calling it "an important step in the fight to get big money out of politics." </p><p>"We've already seen AIPAC, crypto and AI industries flood our elections with millions in obscure ads — and we must push back," Warren said on X. </p><p>AIPAC also heaped praise on the vote.</p><p>"The DNC made clear today that all Democrats, including millions who are AIPAC members, have the right to participate fully in the democratic process," AIPAC spokesman Deryn Sousa said. "And we plan to do just that."</p><p>DNC Chair Ken Paxton pushed back on criticism of the AIPAC resolution, saying the committee voted to pass a resolution "condemning the corrosive influence of ALL dark money in Democratic primaries." </p><p>"We had various resolutions that focused on different industries and groups, and instead of going one-by-one, we passed a blanket repudiation," Martin said on X. "I have made my position on this clear from day one: We must end the influence of dark money in our politics and restore power back to the people."</p><p>There were at least five PACs related to AIPAC that <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/the-watchdogs/2026/02/27/aipac-pro-israel-groups-chicago-area-democratic-congressional-primaries-miller-conyears-ervin-bean-fine" target="_blank" ><u>tried to influence five congressional races</u></a> in Illinois — and the group saw two victories: Donna Miller in the 2nd and Bean in the 8th. The groups fared worse in the 9th District, spending money to harm Kat Abughazaleh’s chances and trying to boost state Sen. Laura Fine. Evanston Mayor Daniel Biss won instead. One AIPAC-affiliated group <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/2026/03/13/cryptocurrency-ai-join-in-31-million-super-pac-blitz-in-four-congressional-primaries" target="_blank" >spent big dollars</a> to help Chicago City Treasurer Melissa Conyears-Ervin in the 7th and to oppose real estate executive Jason Friedman. State Rep. La Shawn Ford instead <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/elections/2026/03/17/us-house-illinois-7th-congressional-district-primary-results" target="_blank" >won the race</a>.</p><p>Artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency groups also played an outsized factor in Illinois races. Fairshake, the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/elections/2026/03/09/artificial-intelligence-bobby-rush-ad-jesse-jackson-jr-campaign#:~:text=Leading%20the%20Future's%20top%20funder,the%20Trump%20administration%20deport%20people." target="_blank" ><u>pro-cryptocurrency PAC</u></a> with connections to supporters of President Donald Trump, spent about $10 million running negative ads about Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton in the Democratic Senate primary, and $2.5 million against Ford in the 7th District race to replace retiring U.S. Rep. Danny Davis. Both candidates they targeted won comfortably.</p><p>Think Big, the Democratic arm of the pro-AI PAC Leading the Future, spent $1.4 million supporting Jesse Jackson Jr. in the 2nd Congressional District and $1.1 million for Melissa Bean in the 8th. The PAC, funded by OpenAI stakeholders, won one (Bean) and lost one (Jackson).<br></p>
entries.author: Tina Sfondeles

entries.title: NFL under federal investigation for anticompetitive practices as TV costs rise
entries.summary: <p>The Justice Department is investigating the NFL for potential anticompetitive practices, according to a government official.</p><p>The official, who was not authorized to discuss an ongoing investigation by name and spoke on condition of anonymity Thursday, said the investigation is "about affordability for consumers and creating an even playing field for providers."</p><p>The investigation was first reported by The Wall Street Journal.</p><p>The NFL has not received a notification that the league is being investigated, according to two other people with knowledge of the situation. Those people spoke on condition of anonymity because they are not authorized to speak on possible legal matters.</p><p>The investigation comes amid increasing federal scrutiny of the amount of money fans are paying to watch sports on television. The Federal Communications Commission, for example, is seeking public comments on the ongoing shift of live sports from broadcast channels to streaming services.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/sports-media/2026/04/09/sports-fast-channels-chicago-sting-indoor-soccer-masl-scripps-sports-network-womens-sports-network-ryz-sports-network-pickleballtv-stadium-blackhawks-chicago-sports-network-fire-apple-tv-bulls-prime-video" >Looking for sports to watch for free? Here’s a FAST way to do it</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>The NFL said in a statement Thursday that over 87% of its games are available on broadcast television, including all that are played in a team's local market.</p><p>"The NFL's media distribution model is the most fan and broadcaster-friendly in the entire sports and entertainment industry. The 2025 season was our most viewed since 1989 and reflects the strength of the NFL distribution model and its wide availability to all fans," the league said in its statement.</p><p>Utah Sen. Mike Lee, chair of the Senate judiciary subcommittee on antitrust, competition policy, and consumer rights, wrote a letter to the Justice Department and the Federal Trade Commission on March 3 urging them to review whether the NFL's distribution methods are in line with the Sports Broadcasting Act, which grants limited antitrust immunity to allow teams to collectively license game broadcasts to national networks.</p><p>"The modern distribution environment differs substantially from the conditions that precipitated this exemption. Instead of a small number of free broadcast networks, the NFL now licenses games simultaneously to subscription streaming platforms, premium cable networks, and technology companies operating under different business models," the Republican senator wrote. "To the extent collectively licensed game packages are placed behind subscription paywalls, these arrangements may no longer align with the statutory concept of sponsored telecasting or the consumer-access rationale underlying the antitrust exemption."</p><p>Lee said in his letter that football fans spent almost $1,000 on cable and streaming subscriptions. Forbes estimated the cost of watching every NFL game via streaming last season at $765.</p><p>The NFL aired games last season on CBS, NBC, ABC/ESPN/ESPN+, Fox, NFL Network, Amazon Prime Video, Netflix and YouTube TV.</p><p>The Sports Broadcasting Act exemption passed in 1961 applies only to broadcast television. Courts have ruled in the past that it does not apply to other media, including cable, satellite and streaming.</p><p>The Sports Broadcasting Act includes a rule allowing blackouts of local games, which still applies to out-of-market packages sold by the league. The NFL ended local TV blackouts, which applied to games within 75 miles of a team's market if they did not sell out 72 hours before kickoff, after the 2014 season.</p><p>Last year, the House Judiciary Committee requested briefings from the NFL, NBA, NHL and MLB on whether antitrust exemptions should still be granted for coordinating their broadcast television rights.</p><p>All four of the major North American professional sports leagues have deals with streaming platforms.</p><p>In 2024, a jury in U.S. District Court in Los Angeles ruled the NFL violated antitrust laws in distributing out-of-market Sunday afternoon games on a premium subscription service and awarded $4.7 billion in damages.</p><p>A federal judge overturned the verdict in the class-action lawsuit because the testimony of two witnesses for the subscribers had flawed methodologies and should have been excluded.</p><p>The lawsuit covered 2.4 million residential subscribers and 48,000 businesses in the United States who paid for the "Sunday Ticket" package on DirecTV of out-of-market games from the 2011 through 2022 seasons.</p><p>Because damages can be tripled under federal antitrust laws, the NFL could have been liable for more than $14 billion. </p>
entries.author: AP

entries.title: What are the the 400 uses of dental floss?
entries.summary: <p>"Old age isn't a battle," Philip Roth once wrote. "Old age is a massacre."</p><p>One certainly suffers losses. First, friends and loved ones are scythed down by jealous time. Second, your body acts up in ways I shan't catalogue.</p><p>But there are also advantages. For instance, after decades of trying, I've finally gotten good at flossing. In my younger years, I'd begin with determination anew after every visit to the dentist. But weeks would pass, I'd skip a day, then two, and the little white box of floss would be pushed aside in the bathroom, ignored until the next visit.</p><p>Not anymore. Lately, I've been a champion, floss-wise. I'm not sure why — probably with the mounting problems of age, I don't want to lose my teeth too. I almost look forward to flossing, which gives you an idea of how exciting my life has become.</p><p>So when I got to the end of one spool Tuesday night, I immediately toddled off to my wife's bathroom to raid her supply, grabbing a package of GUM Fine Floss — mint, waxed, the good stuff.</p><p>As I opened it, I read this bit of ballyhoo on the package:</p><p>"UP TO 400 USES."</p><p><i>Four hundred ways to use dental floss?!</i> I marveled. That's a lot of uses. I honestly couldn't imagine what they might be. </p><p>Finding your way out of a labyrinth? There had to be some very strange, esoteric, highly amusing suggestions from the GUM folks. I must know.</p><p>Jumping online, I found GUM to be a local establishment — part of SUNSTAR, a Japanese company whose American operation is based in Schaumburg. But no official "GUM 400."</p><p>I wrote to the folks at GUM (an abbreviation of "Gentle Uletic Massage," "uletic" meaning, "pertaining to gums") Tuesday night. Not expecting much. If you remember <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/columnists/2022/5/1/23051827/peanut-butter-taste-roasting-smuckers-public-relations-northwestern-kellogg-roosevelt-georgia" target="_blank" >our bitter experience with Smuckers,</a> trying to get them to explain why their natural peanut butter tastes so good, you'll know that my hopes for any given corporation deigning to comment on any given subject are slim.</p><p>Impatient, I explored online.</p><p>The first hit, "11 Surprising Uses for Dental Floss" by the American Association of Retired Persons (I'm telling you: old people, we love our floss). The first was not what I would call hip: "1. Remove skin tags" Were I composing that list, I'd lead with "7. Detach sticky cookies." From baking sheets if — what? — your spatula is broken? </p><p>The problem with the AARP list is, it's all notional. Is there anyone who actually uses dental floss to slice cheesecakes? (Answer: yes. YouTube offers many videos of cheesecakes being smartly cut with dental floss "No drag, no mess, perfection," says Chef Dave Martin. Eli's Cheesecake does not use floss to cut their cheesecakes — the crispy shortbread crust interferes — but does use it to slice unbaked pies).</p><p>The internet is alive with lists of ways to use dental floss other than to clean between your teeth. Hawaii's Kaua’i Hiking Tours offers "27 Survival Uses For Dental Floss," starting with (AARP take note) "1. Make a Lean-To" and including clotheslines, thread, shoelaces, and my favorite, as "dummy cord": a secure line to keep your knife or compass from tumbling out of your backpack and being lost.</p><p>But I wanted real emergency uses. Documented cases. Which led quickly to the realm of prison escape. In 2012, two bank robbers climbed down from the 17th floor of Chicago's Metropolitan Correctional Center using rope fashioned from bedsheets and floss. In his book "Saving Justice," former FBI director James Comey explains how two drug dealers and a bomb maker escaped from their seventh floor jail cell in Manhattan using an unbreakable dental floss rope "impossibly strong and dangerously thin." (Practical tip when using dental floss ropes: Remember to wear heavy gloves).</p><p>I should have made this a week-long series. The end looms and I haven't even mentioned Frank Zappa's deathless lyric, "Movin' to Montana soon/Gonna be a dental floss tycoon." Nor provided this revelatory exchange with my wife.</p><p>Tearing myself away from online lists, I showed her the GUM package, giddy with pride.</p><p>"Let's play, 'You be the columnist,'" I announced. "Where's the column in that?"</p><p>She carefully examined the small white and green package.</p><p>"'Up to 400 uses,'" she read.</p><p>"Exactly,' I began. "What COULD those uses <i>be</i>?!?"</p><p>She gave me a pitying look.</p><p>"They mean 'uses' as in occasions using dental floss," she said quietly. "This package will allow you to floss 400 times."</p><p>Oh. I sagged, struggling to dismiss her perspective as mistaken, certainly not as thrilling as the rabbit hole I had just plunged down. But she's right. She's always right. Makes a person sometimes challenging to live with, to be honest. But we get by. Another consolation of age.</p>
entries.author: Neil Steinberg

entries.title: Migrantes de Chicago enfrentan estafas, rentas exorbitantes y condiciones peligrosas en su búsqueda de vivienda
entries.summary: <p>En una ventosa tarde de jueves en febrero, Daniela y su familia, cuyas edades van de 9 a 35 años, no pensaban en la cena ni en las tareas escolares.</p><p>Estaban metiendo frenéticamente ropa, zapatos y otros artículos personales en bolsas, desesperados por desocupar su departamento de dos habitaciones en el vecindario de South Shore antes de que se metiera el sol.</p><p>El caos había comenzado más temprano esa semana con un golpe en la puerta. Una mujer que afirmaba representar a Atlas Asset Management, empresa que supervisa el edificio de tres pisos en el que vivían, estaba en la puerta, preguntando quiénes eran y a quién estaban pagando la renta.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-be0000" name="module-be0000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><h3>La Voz Chicago WhatsApp</h3>Encuentra más noticias en nuestro canal de WhatsApp. <a class="Link" href="https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb0XT1XCHDylm3rCs40P" target="_blank" ><b>Síguenos.</b></a></div> </div><p>“Dijo que este departamento debía estar desocupado, que teníamos que irnos y que la policía estaba en camino”, dijo Daniela.</p><p>Daniela y su familia son de Venezuela y ya no tienen <a class="Link" href="https://www.wbez.org/reset-with-sasha-ann-simons/2025/03/04/fears-rise-for-venezuelan-chicagoans-after-trump-rescinds-temporary-protected-status" target="_blank" >un estatus legal temporal</a> en Estados Unidos. WBEZ no publica sus nombres completos porque la familia teme la deportación.</p><p>Sin que la familia lo supiera, eran ocupantes ilegales en un edificio que había cambiado de dueño recientemente, siendo víctimas de una estafa. Un agente de arrendamiento fraudulento había entrado a unidades vacías de un edificio en 79th Street y Paxton Avenue y las había alquilado a familias migrantes desesperadas como la de Daniela.</p><p>Cuando llegó el oficial de la policía, el mensaje fue directo: la familia tenía una semana para salir.</p><p>Encontrar una vivienda segura y adecuada se ha vuelto más complicado en Chicago, ya que los migrantes enfrentan estatus legal precario debido a los cambios en las políticas federales bajo la administración del presidente Donald Trump.</p><p>Su falta de ingresos estables y miedo a la deportación los han hecho más vulnerables a este tipo de estafas de vivienda. También deben lidiar con rentas exorbitantes y una escasez de viviendas asequibles.</p><p>Y una nueva ley estatal que entró en vigor en enero facilita a los dueños de propiedades llamar a la policía y desalojar a los ocupantes ilegales sin un proceso de desalojo prolongado, poniendo a familias migrantes como el de Daniela en riesgo de quedarse sin vivienda.</p><div class="Enhancement" class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Daniela empaca algunas de sus pertenencias el 5 de febrero, la noche en que su familia dejó un apartamento en 79th Street y Paxton Avenue.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Esther Yoon-Ji Kang/WBEZ</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p></p><h3>Buscando un hogar estable</h3><p>Desde su llegada a Chicago en 2023, la familia se ha mudado más de cinco veces. Después de quedarse en un albergue para migrantes durante meses, recibieron un vale temporal de asistencia para la renta del estado y encontraron una casa que albergaba a los seis miembros por casi $2,000 al mes.</p><p>Pero la estabilidad fue efímera.</p><p>En seis meses, los pagos del vale temporal expiraron, y con solo uno de los adultos con un trabajo estable, no pudieron continuar pagando la renta.</p><p>“Durante esos seis meses, salí todos los días, recorriendo calle por calle buscando trabajo y no encontré nada”, dijo Pedro, el compañero de Daniela.</p><p>Sin empleo, Daniela comentó que estaban desesperados por dinero y mendigaban en las calles con su hijo de 4 años, incluso en fríos días de invierno.</p><p>Eventualmente, la familia tuvo que abandonar el espacioso hogar que pagaba con el vale. Se agruparon con amigos y conocidos en casas separadas hasta que la familia logró acomodar a seis personas en un departamento de una sola habitación.</p><p>“Mi mamá, su pareja y dos hijos compartían una habitación”, dijo Daniela. “Yo, mi pareja y mi hijo solíamos dormir en la sala, separados por una cortina”.</p><p>Estaban amontonados.</p><p>Así que en noviembre de 2025, cuando un hombre que hablaba español con fluidez les ofreció un departamento de dos habitaciones por $1,000 —solo un poco más de lo que estaban pagando por su departamento de una habitación— aprovecharon la oportunidad, ignorando varias señales de alerta.</p><p>El hombre, que decía ser el administrador del edificio, nunca les dio su nombre completo. Nunca recibieron un contrato de arrendamiento ni recibos al pagar la renta en efectivo. Todo lo que tenían era un acuerdo informal.</p><p>Además de todo, el departamento estaba plagado de peligros, entre ellos una puerta principal que no se cerraba con llave, la falta de calefacción central y la pintura pelada.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-4b59fb" name="image-4b59fb"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/3cdd03d/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3995x2242+0+377/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F00%2F69%2F85c9ee5c4450b5dcb7a3d267f084%2Fimg-6677.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/91f7ed1/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3995x2242+0+377/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F00%2F69%2F85c9ee5c4450b5dcb7a3d267f084%2Fimg-6677.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/f8786da/2147483647/strip/true/crop/3995x2242+0+377/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F00%2F69%2F85c9ee5c4450b5dcb7a3d267f084%2Fimg-6677.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="El apartamento en 7900 S. Paxton estaba lleno de peligros, incluyendo una puerta de entrada que no se cerraba con llave, la falta de calefacción central y la pintura pelada.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Esther Yoon-Ji Kang/WBEZ</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>A pesar de las condiciones miserables, lo llamaron hogar. Arreglaron lo que pudieron, le dieron una mano de pintura nueva y trataron de mantenerlo ordenado.</p><p>Un día antes de que llegara la policía, la sala estaba llena de globos por una fiesta de cumpleaños para uno de los niños.</p><p>La fiesta fue la última vez que Daniela y su familia se sentían felices y seguros en su hogar.</p><p>Según defensores de la vivienda, la búsqueda de un lugar para vivir por parte de la familia refleja la de miles de migrantes en la zona.</p><p>“Lo hemos visto muchas veces”, dijo Michelle Gilbert, directora legal y de políticas del Centro Legal para Mejorar la Vivienda.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-c0c30c" name="image-c0c30c"></a> <picture
